design_philosophy.core_loop: Select track, manage car setup (tire hardness, wing angle, transmission), race against AI opponents, overtake for points, manage tire wear and engine heat
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balance between speed and vehicle durability; managing tire wear mid-race and preventing engine overheat through careful gear management
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete as a Formula One driver in a fully licensed championship with authentic 1993 season drivers and teams
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes speed and arcade accessibility over realistic F1 physics; tracks filled with obstacles (signs, adboards, platforms) to artificially increase challenge rather than relying on simulation depth; supports both single-player campaign and arcade scoring modes

difficulty.checkpointing: Single race format with pit stop requirements; forced quit if driver fails to pit for tire replacement or overheats engine
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Four selectable difficulty levels (Novice, Amateur, Professional, Expert) that scale both AI competitor speed and car damage tolerance; player can configure championship length (1-12 tracks)

progression.upgrade_system: Car setup configuration: tire hardness adjustment, wing angle, transmission settings adjusted before and after races
progression.unlock_mechanism: Championship mode allows player to select which tracks to race and in what order; four difficulty tiers unlock gradually based on player skill
replayability_hooks: Four difficulty levels with increasing AI speed and damage tolerance
Customizable championship (any combination of 12 available tracks)
Arcade mode with points for overtaking/running vs. points lost for being overtaken
Turbo mode with altered visual presentation and increased speed

world_building_techniques: Fully licensed 1993 F1 season with authentic drivers, teams, and track layouts
Player drives for fictional Domark team (with producer James Tripp as player character)
Authentic pit crew presence and mechanical failures (tire wear, engine overheating)

faction_indicators: Licensed F1 teams: Benetton, Ferrari, Ligier, Lotus, McLaren, Tyrrell, Williams
Rivalry with named drivers: Schumacher, Patrese, Alesi, Berger, Hill, Prost, etc.
danger_signals: Track obstacles (signs, adboards, platforms) create collision hazards
Tire wear requiring pit stop strategy
environmental_storytelling: 12 authentic F1 circuits with correct 1993 layouts
Retired vehicles visible on Silverstone track
Pit crew presence indicates real mechanical simulation elements
